lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The lot behind the bay is one lamp and three cars. Maeve's sedan is in the far corner, where she has always parked it — habit from the years when she needed the distance from the door.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She sits. She counts. Four years teaches you to count: days clean, minutes between cravings, the gap between the craving and the next thing you manage to do instead. She is very good at counting.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The bottle has been in the center console since June. She put it there after a call she remembers only by feel. She moved it twice, thought about discarding it, set it back. Tonight the pediatric case broke wrong and the child survived and she held the parents up with both hands and told them everything would be all right, and they believed her, and she was the last steady thing in that room, and then the room ended.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: One drink. She holds it. Sets the cap back. Places the bottle where it was.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Four years, two months, eleven days. The number falls.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She is aware of herself precisely — hands on the wheel, the cold seam of the window against her shoulder, the changed quality of her own breathing. She has already begun building the silence she intends to keep. It will not be difficult. She has built silences before.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: When she crosses back toward the building, Dana is coming out the side door with a runsheet and a cold coffee, reading as she walks.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Dana looks up. Motion caught at the edge of sight — the old reflex.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Their eyes meet. Brief, exact, and not quite deniable.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Dana reads the flatness in Maeve's face. The room-closed-from-inside quality. She has seen it before, in other people, in other hospitals, and she knows what it is called.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Maeve goes in. Dana finishes the runsheet. Takes the long corridor to the board so they arrive from different directions.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The shift continues. The ER absorbs her back without ceremony — completely, indifferently, without asking what just happened in the lot.
